channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: San Francisco celebrates iconic peace activist Wavy Gravy's 90th birthday
item.description: The peace activist, counterculture icon and philanthropist known as Wavy Gravy turned 90, and San Francisco threw a big party.
item.content:encoded: The peace activist, counterculture icon and philanthropist known as Wavy Gravy turned 90, and San Francisco threw a big party.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Monahan

item.title: Prayer festival calls for more religion in politics, not less
item.description: A prayer festival on the National Mall in Washington, DC was partially organized and funded by the federal government. Its evangelical messaging called for more religion in politics not less. Critics are concerned about an erosion of the wall between church and state. NPR's Emily Feng went to the event to understand the audience and appetite for this approach.
item.content:encoded: A prayer festival on the National Mall in Washington, DC was partially organized and funded by the federal government. Its evangelical messaging called for more religion in politics not less. Critics are concerned about an erosion of the wall between church and state. NPR's Emily Feng went to the event to understand the audience and appetite for this approach.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Cineplexity: the best and worst of wedding movies
item.description: In this week's Cineplexity, where we examine life's complexities by talking about cinema, we're taking on the best and worst of the beloved trope of "weddings in the movies."
item.content:encoded: In this week's Cineplexity, where we examine life's complexities by talking about cinema, we're taking on the best and worst of the beloved trope of "weddings in the movies."
item.dc:creator: Marc Rivers

item.title: What motivated Chinese-born Uyghers to fight in Syria's civil war
item.description: Thousands of Chinese-born Uyghur fighters fled China to fight in the Syrian civil war. They say generations of political persecution in China motivated them to take up arms.
item.content:encoded: Thousands of Chinese-born Uyghur fighters fled China to fight in the Syrian civil war. They say generations of political persecution in China motivated them to take up arms.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: What scientists are learning about geoengineering from satellite pollution
item.description: Professor Eloise Marais from the University College London talks about her research on pollution from satellites and its impact on earth's climate.
item.content:encoded: Professor Eloise Marais from the University College London talks about her research on pollution from satellites and its impact on earth's climate.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: What Louisiana Sen. Bill Cassidy's primary loss says about Trump's grip on the GOP
item.description: GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy's primary loss in Louisiana shows the power of President Trump's opposition. It also highlights the importance of voting rules and maps.
item.content:encoded: GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy's primary loss in Louisiana shows the power of President Trump's opposition. It also highlights the importance of voting rules and maps.
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Election day confusion in Louisiana after voting changes
item.description: The governor of Louisiana canceled the U.S. House primaries after tens of thousands of votes had already been cast. On Election Day, we hear from voters trying to make sense of the last-minute changes.
item.content:encoded: The governor of Louisiana canceled the U.S. House primaries after tens of thousands of votes had already been cast. On Election Day, we hear from voters trying to make sense of the last-minute changes.
item.dc:creator: Acacia Squires

item.title: Iranian man barely survives bombing, but wants war to go on
item.description: An Iranian man who was buried under rubble in US Israel bombing campaign says he wants the war to go on, and destroy the regime.
item.content:encoded: An Iranian man who was buried under rubble in US Israel bombing campaign says he wants the war to go on, and destroy the regime.
item.dc:creator: Durrie Bouscaren

item.title: Understanding modern China through reporting on a hidden family history
item.description: While in China to cover President Trump's visit to meet with leader Xi Jinping, host of NPR and WBUR's Here & Now Scott Tong spent time learning about a difficult chapter in his family's history.
item.content:encoded: While in China to cover President Trump's visit to meet with leader Xi Jinping, host of NPR and WBUR's Here & Now Scott Tong spent time learning about a difficult chapter in his family's history.
item.dc:creator: Scott Tong

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: producing a great interview
item.description: The team behind NPR's Wild Card explains how careful preparation helps them produce interviews that reveal deep and surprising human moments.
item.content:encoded: The team behind NPR's Wild Card explains how careful preparation helps them produce interviews that reveal deep and surprising human moments.
item.dc:creator: Fiona Geiran
